name: Validity Conditions Claim Hardening Update
summary: Follow-up claim-support update that adds the published validity-condition findings and sources to the affected SSR baseline claim support maps after the controlled-comparison bundle was published.

scope.research_question: How should the published validity-condition evidence be reflected in the affected baseline claim support maps?

proposed_changes.summary: Add validity-condition realism and authenticity findings to the quality-evaluation support map.
proposed_changes.rationale: The published Nguyen/Cao, Cao et al., SMART, and Wu et al. findings add direct support for multidimensional realism metrics.

proposed_changes.summary: Add controlled model/workflow comparison findings to the model-generation support map.
proposed_changes.rationale: The new evidence strengthens the task-bound model/workflow sensitivity claim without changing claim confidence.

proposed_changes.summary: Add lesson-context and workflow-prompting evidence to the prompt-engineering support map.
proposed_changes.rationale: The new studies add bounded evidence that contextual prompting and workflow prompts shape synthetic response behavior.

proposed_changes.summary: Add new real-response and comparator-anchor findings to the real-response-comparison support map.
proposed_changes.rationale: The new evidence clarifies comparator types: lesson ideas, expert-coded authenticity, IRT item difficulty, and programming learning records.

proposed_changes.summary: Add the SMART scoring-adjacent item-difficulty boundary finding to the scorer-validation-use support map.
proposed_changes.rationale: SMART is validity-relevant to scoring workflows but must remain classified as item-development/difficulty-estimation evidence, not synthetic-only final scorer validation.

proposed_changes.summary: Add the SMART item-development acceptable-use boundary to the acceptable-scoring-uses support map.
proposed_changes.rationale: The published finding supports a bounded calibration/development use category when synthetic responses are anchored to human-scored real-response IRT evidence.

proposed_claim_implications.note: Adds direct support for grade-level, cognitive-authenticity, item-difficulty, and error-pattern realism metrics.
Adds direct support for model/workflow sensitivity across multi-model, DPO, multi-agent, IRT-aligned, and cognitive-prototype approaches.
Adds explicit comparator anchors from lesson ideas, real utterance/authenticity data, human-scored IRT estimates, and programming learning records.
Adds SMART as scoring-adjacent item-difficulty evidence while preserving the synthetic-only validation boundary.

next_actions: Publication complete. Review downstream pages if this change affects shared surfaces.
applicability_boundary: Retain the current applicability and limitation language.
construct_mapping: Retain the explicit limitations attached to each new support entry.
method_classification: Keep the staged task-bound language.
scorer_validation_relevance: Keep SMART's support_role as adjacent/direct bounded evidence according to the claim target, with explicit limitations.
source_fidelity: Keep the source IDs and finding IDs limited to the published validity-condition records.
synthesis_overreach: Publish the staged support-map updates as written.
